methodology_sections: 1.1 Asset Inventory - Financial assets, state assets, privilege assets, reputation assets
1.3 System Architecture Mapping - Contract hierarchies, dependencies, upgrade patterns, access control
2.1 Entry Point Enumeration - Public/external functions, callbacks, parameter analysis

financial_assets: None (pure math libraries; no ETH/ERC20/NFT custody)
Indirect financial exposure: any upstream protocol using these routines for pricing, interest, collateral ratios, liquidation math, fee math
state_assets: None (no contract storage; only constants and transient memory/stack values)
Type-level encoding of packedFloat (bit-packed mantissa/sign/exponent/precision-flag) used as a value object across the system
privilege_assets: None (no ownership, roles, pausing, upgrades, or admin-controlled parameters)
reputation_assets: Correctness and determinism of arithmetic outputs (protocol trust depends on absence of precision/normalization errors)
ULP-tolerance adherence (especially sqrt and ln) affecting integrators' risk assumptions

system_architecture.inheritance_hierarchy: No inheritance. Float128 and Ln are Solidity libraries. Types.sol defines the custom value type packedFloat.
external_dependencies: Solidity inline assembly extensively used for bit operations and arithmetic
system_architecture.upgrade_patterns: None. Libraries only; no proxy/diamond patterns and no initializers.
system_architecture.access_control_structure: None. No roles/ownership/modifiers. Note: Ln exposes public pure methods callable by any external contract/user, but they do not mutate state.

external_calls: recursive call to Ln.ln(...) inside ln_helper (public function invoked internally)

upgradeability_surface.notes: Solidity library with only constants and internal pure functions; no storage.
Solidity library exposing public pure functions (ln/calculateQ1/Q2/Q3); no storage.
Defines custom value type packedFloat only.
